channel.title: NYT > World News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Iran’s Revolutionary Guards: The Spine of a Militarized State
item.description: With their pervasive military, political and economic clout, the Guards are often considered the main impediment to regime change, or any change, in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Neil MacFarquhar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps members in Tehran in 2024, commemorating Palestinian and Lebanese militants killed by Israel.

item.title: Gen Z-Backed Rapper Is on Course to Lead Nepal With Landslide Win
item.description: A youth-led uprising brought Nepal’s government down last year. Now, a 35-year-old politician demanding change is set to become prime minister.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Beech, Sajal Pradhan, Binod Ghimire and Hannah Reyes Morales
item.media:credit: Hannah Reyes Morales for The New York Times
item.media:description: Balendra Shah, center, during his campaign to become the next prime minister of Nepal last Saturday. To his right is Rabi Lamichhane, the Rastriya Swatantra Party president.

item.title: A Loose Band of Emerging Powers Is Divided Over Iran
item.description: Brazil, China and Russia all denounced the U.S.-Israeli attacks, but other nations in the BRICS group haven’t, even though Iran is a fellow member.
item.dc:creator: Ana Ionova
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eraldo Peres/Associated Press
item.media:description: Premier Li Qiang of China, Prime Minister Narendra Modi of India, President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva of Brazil and President Cyril Ramaphosa of South Africa at a BRICS summit in Rio de Janeiro last year.

item.title: Ahead of Colombia’s Elections, Violence on the Campaign Trail Rattles Politicians
item.description: Politicians in Colombia have increasingly become targets of violence. A rise in kidnappings, death threats and assassinations has shaken the country ahead of the vote.
item.dc:creator: Luis Ferré-Sadurní and Federico Rios
item.media:credit: Federico Rios for The New York Times
item.media:description: Police officers deployed across Popayan, Colombia, are monitoring security ahead of legislative elections on Sunday.

item.title: In Russia’s Former Capital, Restoring the Past to Survive the Present
item.description: With political activism off-limits, residents of St. Petersburg are finding purpose and community in the “politics of small deeds,” repairing and cleaning architectural treasures.
item.dc:creator: Ivan Nechepurenko and Mary Gelman
item.media:credit: Mary Gelman for The New York Times

item.title: In War’s First Week, a Punishing Military Campaign With No Coherent Endgame
item.description: The U.S. and Israel have pounded Iran’s leadership and undercut its defense capabilities, but President Trump has offered wildly different explanations for what he hopes to achieve.
item.dc:creator: Mark Mazzetti, Tyler Pager, Ronen Bergman, Farnaz Fassihi, Eric Schmitt, Erika Solomon and Julian E. Barnes
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A huge plume of smoke has rose in the sky after US-Israel airstrikes in the west of Tehran, on Tuesday.

item.title: Trump Assembles a New Coalition to ‘Eradicate’ Cartels
item.description: At a gathering in Florida, the president asked the leaders of a dozen Latin American and Caribbean nations to help the U.S. military crush armed trafficking groups.
item.dc:creator: Annie Correal and Shawn McCreesh
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump with Latin American and Caribbean leaders at the summit on Saturday. U.S. officials said a new coalition would aggressively target cartels and organized crime across the region.

item.title: Pakistan Praised Trump. Now It Risks Being Caught Up in His War With Iran
item.description: U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran have set off a crippling energy crisis and sparked deadly protests in one of the world’s most populous Muslim nations.
item.dc:creator: Elian Peltier
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Aamir Qureshi/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Crowds protesting the U.S.-Israeli bombing campaign against Iran, in Islamabad, Pakistan, on Friday.

item.title: Europe Didn’t Want War With Iran. But So Far, It Can’t Stay Out of It.
item.description: From London to Rome and beyond, leaders are facing diplomatic headwinds and criticism at home as they take part in a conflict they did not seek.
item.dc:creator: Mark Landler
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Sebastien Bozon/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: President Emmanuel Macron has declared that France is not at war. But he ordered the aircraft carrier Charles de Gaulle to the eastern Mediterranean Sea.

item.title: Russia Attacks Kharkiv and Kyiv in Ukraine
item.description: At least 10 people were killed when a Russian missile hit a five-story apartment building in the city of Kharkiv, and Kyiv and several other regions also came under attack.
item.dc:creator: Cassandra Vinograd
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Vyacheslav Madiyevskyy/Reuters
item.media:description: Emergency workers removing the body of a victim from the site of an overnight strike on an apartment building in Kharkiv, Ukraine, on Saturday.

item.title: How Good Intentions Helped Pave Trump’s Road to Iran
item.description: Humanitarians proposed a loophole in international law. Decades later, Trump is jumping through it.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Taub
item.category: War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Luca Bruno/Associated Press
item.media:description: Iranian expatriates around the world urged the Trump administration to intervene in Iran as the death toll from the protests rose in January.

item.title: For Xi, Trump’s Embrace of War Proves China Needs More Power
item.description: China is learning lessons from the war in Iran. Chief among them, the United States may pose an even greater threat than Beijing thought.
item.dc:creator: David Pierson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Maxim Shemetov/Reuters
item.media:description: Xi Jinping, China’s top leader, during an annual legislative meeting in Beijing on Wednesday.

item.title: Surfers in Munich (Yes, Munich) Just Want Their Wave Back
item.description: For decades, enthusiasts rode the cresting Eisbach creek in the southern German city, some 200 miles from the nearest coast. But the wave has vanished, prompting arguments about how to restore it.
item.dc:creator: Christopher F. Schuetze and Laetitia Vançon
item.media:credit: Laetitia Vancon for The New York Times

item.title: Lebanon at ‘Tipping Point’ as It Seeks to Disarm Hezbollah
item.description: The country is waiting to see if the government seizes on this moment to disarm the Iranian-backed armed group and how the militants will respond.
item.dc:creator: Christina Goldbaum
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Hassan Ammar/Associated Press
item.media:description: Hezbollah supporters gathering in the southern suburb of Beirut, Lebanon, on Sunday to mourn the death of Iran’s supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

item.title: He Had a Purple Heart, PTSD and a Rap Sheet. He Had to Leave the U.S.
item.description: After a long battle with drug addiction, Sae Joon Park felt settled in a new life. But he was deported last year and is now fighting to get back to Hawaii.
item.dc:creator: Max Kim
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Jean Chung for The New York Times
item.media:description: Sae Joon Park in his apartment in Busan, South Korea, in February. Despite living in the U.S. for nearly 50 years, Park was ordered to move back to South Korea in June 2025.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi and Yan Zhuang

item.title: Stocks Tank in Asia as Oil Price Surges
item.description: Asian markets tumbled on Monday as Middle East tensions and spiking oil prices sparked a mass exit from the region’s previously booming stocks.
item.dc:creator: River Akira Davis and Meaghan Tobin
item.media:credit: Kazuhiro Nogi/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images

item.title: On the Road With Zelensky, Weathered, Weary and Fighting On
item.description: Ukraine’s leader, Volodymyr Zelensky, traveled east to visit frontline troops trying to stave off Russian attacks, and invited reporters for The New York Times to go with him.
item.dc:creator: Kim Barker and Brendan Hoffman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine in Kramatorsk on Friday. “They need a lot of things,” he said of the soldiers. “And each day needs are changing.”

item.title: Ukraine Sent Drone Experts to Protect U.S. Bases in Jordan, Zelensky Says
item.description: As the war in Iran spreads, Kyiv is eagerly offering its hard-won expertise and advanced technology to counter Iranian drones.
item.dc:creator: Kim Barker
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Tyler Hicks/The New York Times
item.media:description: Soldiers in the Donetsk region of Ukraine in December, monitoring the sky to shoot down Russian Shahed drones using a 30-millimeter antiaircraft gun.

item.title: Is the Trial of Erdogan’s Top Rival More About Corruption or Politics?
item.description: Prosecutors accuse the former Istanbul mayor Ekrem Imamoglu of leading a criminal scheme. His supporters say Turkey’s president is trying to eliminate a political foe.
item.dc:creator: Ben Hubbard, Gulsin Harman and Safak Timur
item.media:credit: Burak Kara/Getty Images
item.media:description: Demonstrators in Istanbul showing support for Ekrem Imamoglu, then the mayor of Istanbul, last March after he was arrested.

item.title: The Suburb That Won’t Sleep
item.description: Food is bringing thousands of people together to an unexpected place: a plaza west of Toronto. Some fear it’s tearing a neighborhood apart.
item.dc:creator: Vjosa Isai
item.media:credit: Ian Willms for The New York Times

item.title: For China, Billions of Dollars Are at Risk From a Widening War
item.description: The country found a home in the Middle East for its investments and growing markets for steel, electric vehicles and solar panels. Those are now at stake.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Stevenson and Murphy Zhao
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: The New York Times
item.media:description: Beilun Port in China. The Middle East became even more important to China after China’s trade rivalry with the United States escalated.

item.title: Large Fire in Glasgow Forces Closure of Major Train Station
item.description: The blaze that started near Scotland’s busiest station was under control, but officials said they expected train service to be disrupted through Monday.
item.dc:creator: Yan Zhuang
item.media:credit: Jeff J Mitchell/Getty Images
item.media:description: More than 60 firefighters battled the blaze after it began at a shop near Glasgow’s main train station on Sunday.

item.title: Democrats Seize on Oil Topping $100 a Barrel, as Trump Downplays Jump
item.description: The global oil benchmark topped $100 a barrel late Sunday, putting Republicans on the defensive on an issue at the center of this year’s midterm elections.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Ken Cedeno/Reuters
item.media:description: President Trump immediately sought to downplay the spike in oil prices.

item.title: U.S. Tomahawk Hit Naval Base Beside Iranian School, Video Shows
item.description: The evidence contradicts President Trump’s claim that Iran was responsible for a strike at the school that killed 175 people, most of them children.
item.dc:creator: Malachy Browne and John Ismay
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: Iran’s Security Establishment Celebrates Mojtaba Khamenei’s Selection
item.description: Opponents of the government, however, worried the new supreme leader, a son of the recently killed Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, would rule with an iron fist like his father.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A poster of Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei in Tehran on the night he was announced as the new supreme leader of Iran.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the Conflict on Sunday
item.description: Iran named Mojtaba Khamenei, a son of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, as his father’s successor. The Pentagon announced the death of a seventh U.S. service member.
item.dc:creator: Anushka Patil
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Smoke plumes rising from an oil storage site in Tehran, the Iranian capital, on Sunday.

item.title: This is what happened on March 8.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: Who Is Mojtaba Khamenei, Iran’s New Supreme Leader?
item.description: The selection of a son of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed in a strike on his compound at the start of the war, could anger Iranians seeking change.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni, Sanam Mahoozi and Amelia Nierenberg
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Office of The Iranian Supreme Leader/Wana News Agency, via Reuters
item.media:description: A handout image from Iran state media of Mojtaba Khamenei, a son of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, in Tehran in 2024.

item.title: Iran Has a New Supreme Leader. What Does That Mean?
item.description: Mojtaba Khamenei takes on a role that makes him not only Iran’s spiritual leader but also the highest authority in the land.
item.dc:creator: Carlotta Gall
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Rouzbeh Fouladi/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Mojtaba Khamenei, center, at a rally in Tehran in 2019.

item.title: Sizing Up Iran’s Elite Guards
item.description: Many see the Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps as the main barrier to regime change, or any change at all, in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times

item.title: Mojtaba Khamenei, Son of Iran’s Slain Supreme Leader, Is a Mysterious Figure
item.description: The succession of the slain leader’s son is seen as a signal of the Islamic republic’s defiance of Israel and the United States, and of continuity during crisis.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Rouzbeh Fouladi/Middle East Images, via Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Mojtaba Khamenei, center, the son of the Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, in 2019.

item.title: State Dept. Said to Order Diplomats in Saudi Arabia to Leave
item.description: The ordered departure of U.S. employees in the kingdom indicates that senior diplomats are bracing for a possible surge in violence in the war with Iran, officials say.
item.dc:creator: Edward Wong, Mark Mazzetti, Vivian Nereim and Adam Rasgon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: -/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: The U.S. Embassy in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia.

item.title: Pentagon Announces Seventh U.S. Death in War With Iran
item.description: The service member killed was not publicly identified, but U.S. Central Command said the death was caused by injuries after an attack on a Saudi military base.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt and Helene Cooper
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
item.media:description: The first six American service members killed in the war with Iran were returned to Dover Air Force Base in Delaware on Saturday.

item.title: U.S. Continues Airstrikes Against a Wide Array of Targets in Iran
item.description: A senior U.S. military official said those targets included missile launchers and air-defense sites.
item.dc:creator: John Ismay, Eric Schmitt and Johnatan Reiss
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Smoke rising in Tehran on Sunday after airstrikes.

item.title: Lebanese Villagers Bury Hezbollah Fighters, Praising Their Defiance of Israel
item.description: After a fierce ground fight between Israeli forces and Iran-backed Hezbollah, the village of Nabi Sheet mourned its dead. “We are not here to bow,” said one villager. “We will not kneel.”
item.dc:creator: Sarah Chaayto, David Guttenfelder and Amelia Nierenberg
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: David Guttenfelder/The New York Times
item.media:description: Mourners at a funeral in Nabi Sheet, Lebanon.

item.title: West Bank Violence Intensifies, as Israeli Settlers Kill 3 Palestinians
item.description: A rise in settler attacks is terrorizing Palestinians in the occupied territory, at a time when global attention is focused on the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger, Fatima AbdulKarim and Natan Odenheimer
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Hazem Bader/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Mourners attending the funeral on Sunday for a man who was killed during an Israeli settler attack in Wadi al-Rakhim, in the southern West Bank.

item.title: Israel Strikes Energy Infrastructure Crucial to Iran’s Well-Being
item.description: Continued attacks could make the Iranian government and the country as a whole more vulnerable.
item.dc:creator: Raja Abdulrahim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Smoke billowing from an oil storage facility in Tehran on Sunday. Iran relies heavily on its energy industry.

item.title: Israeli Strikes on Fuel Depots Send Black Clouds Over Iranian Capital
item.description: Immense dark plumes of smoke enveloped Tehran after the Israeli military expanded attacks on energy infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Kiana Hayeri and Parin Behrooz
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times

item.title: U.S. Embassy in Baghdad Targeted as Iraq Gets Drawn Deeper Into Regional War
item.description: Iraq is caught between two allies as the Trump administration pressures Iraqi leaders to distance themselves from Iran.
item.dc:creator: Raja Abdulrahim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Ahmad Al-Rubaye/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: The U.S. Embassy in Iraq, in Baghdad’s fortified Green Zone, on Sunday.

item.title: Iran Says It’s Close to Naming a New Supreme Leader
item.description: Iranian state media reported that the country’s top clerics have identified who will replace Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. They have not released his name.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon and Ravi Mattu
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A man holds a picture of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei during a gathering in Tehran last week to mourn his killing.

item.title: Oslo Police Investigate Explosion Outside U.S. Embassy
item.description: A motive for the explosion was unclear as of Sunday morning, but the police were investigating and said it appeared to be a “targeted attack.”
item.dc:creator: Henrik Pryser Libell and Claire Moses
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Hans O. Torgersen/NTB Scanpix, via Associated Press
item.media:description: Norwegian police and technicians at the entrance of the United States Embassy in Oslo after a loud bang was reported on Sunday.

item.title: Vital Water Desalination Plants in Iran and Bahrain Are Attacked
item.description: Strikes on nonmilitary infrastructure were a “serious escalation,” analysts said, and could widen the war’s impact on civilians.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nereim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: RuelleRuelle/Universal Images Group, via Getty Images
item.media:description: A view of Bahrain from the window of a plane in 2023. Persian Gulf countries depend heavily on desalination technology.

item.title: Trump Executive Order Protected Weedkiller Roundup and a Munition, White Phosphorus
item.description: Citing national security, an unusual executive order gave protection to the herbicide Roundup. It also protected the U.S.’s only supply of a controversial, highly flammable munition.
item.dc:creator: Hiroko Tabuchi
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Tyler Hicks/The New York Times
item.media:description: U.S. military personnel with a round of white phosphorus in Afghanistan in 2011.

item.title: Here is the latest.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi, Edward Wong, Mark Mazzetti, Eric Schmitt and Aaron Boxerman

item.title: Israel Strikes Oil Facilities in Iran
item.description: Huge flames erupted into the sky over Tehran and a nearby city on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi and Devon Lum
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Explosions erupted after the Israeli military struck several fuel storage complexes in Tehran on Saturday.

item.title: Iran’s De Facto Leader Says Iran Will Not Surrender or Stop Its Attacks
item.description: Ali Larijani, the head of Iran’s Supreme National Security Council and a close confidant of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, said Iran was determined to avenge the killing of the leader.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Ali Larijani, secretary of the Supreme National Security Council of Iran, during a news conference in 2024.

item.title: This is what happened on March 7.
What the U.S. and Israel Have Targeted in Their Iran Blitz
item.dc:creator: Adam Goldman, Samuel Granados, Ronen Bergman and Eric Schmitt

item.title: Deadly Clashes in Lebanon as Israel Renews Search for Missing Airman
item.description: Dozens were killed in the Bekaa Valley overnight, the Lebanese health authorities said, amid airstrikes and intense violence in the town of Nabi Sheet.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir and David Guttenfelder
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: David Guttenfelder/The New York Times
item.media:description: Residents walking amid the aftermath of deadly strikes in Nabi Sheet, a town in Lebanon’s eastern Bekaa Valley.

item.title: Two Toronto-Area Synagogues Are Struck by Gunfire
item.description: The episodes, which happened late Friday and early Saturday, came after a third synagogue was damaged by gunfire on Monday, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Mark Walker
item.media:credit: Mert Alper Dervis/Anadolu, via Getty Images
item.media:description: The Beth Avraham Yoseph of Toronto Synagogue was damaged by gunfire late on Friday night, according to the mayor of Vaughan, a suburb of Toronto.

item.title: Ian Huntley, Whose Murder of Schoolgirls Appalled Britain, Dies After Prison Attack
item.description: The 2002 deaths of Holly Wells and Jessica Chapman, and the manhunt that led to a local school caretaker, shook the U.K.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Kwai
item.media:credit: Reuters
item.media:description: Ian Huntley in a photo taken during the search for Holly Wells and Jessica Chapman. Mr. Huntley gave interviews to the news media before being charged with killing them.

item.title: Iranian President’s Apology to Neighboring Gulf States Showcases Leadership Rifts
item.description: Masoud Pezeshkian, the Iranian president, apologized for Iranian strikes on Gulf states before backtracking after criticism from other Iranian leaders. Despite his remarks, Iran has continued its attacks.
item.dc:creator: Leily Nikounazar and Aaron Boxerman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Masoud Pezeshkian of Iran at the United Nations General Assembly in New York, in September.

item.title: Recent U.S. Assessment Found an Attack on Iran Unlikely to Result in Regime Change
item.description: Intelligence officials have been skeptical that a popular uprising could dislodge the Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps, which controls much of Iran’s security apparatus.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: People in Tehran on Friday mourning Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, Iran’s supreme leader, who was killed in the U.S.-Israeli strikes. There seems to be wide agreement in the U.S. intelligence community that the theological government in Iran is deeply entrenched.

item.title: Iran’s Navy Is Weakened but U.S. Still Faces Challenges in Strait of Hormuz
item.dc:creator: Christoph Koettl and Malika Khurana

item.title: Hanging Up
item.description: If decreasing dependence on our phones feels impossible, we might benefit from considering what we’d want to fill the space that they occupy.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Kirsch

item.title: Traveling Around the Turbulent World With Mark Carney
item.description: The prime minister visited India, Australia and Japan to sell Canada to foreign investors and call for middle powers to unite, as the United States and Israel attacked Iran.
item.dc:creator: Ian Austen
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Ayush Kumar/Getty Images
item.media:description: Mark Carney let his guard down after a speech in Australia.

item.title: Russia Revels in a Sudden Reversal in Fortunes as Oil and Gas Prices Soar
item.description: President Vladimir V. Putin threatened to cut off remaining gas supplies to Europe as the Iran war drives a surge in energy costs.
item.dc:creator: Paul Sonne and Jeanna Smialek
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Amr Alfiky/Reuters
item.media:description: Oil tankers off the coast of the United Arab Emirates. The conflict in Iran has disrupted oil production across the Middle East.

item.title: War in the Middle East Threatens Global Food Production
item.description: The Persian Gulf is a major source of fertilizers, making the conflict disruptive to the global production of food.
item.dc:creator: Peter S. Goodman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas
item.media:credit: Amit Dave/Reuters
item.media:description: Workers sprinkling fertilizer in a paddy field in India, which buys some 40 percent of its urea and phosphate-based fertilizers from the Middle East.
